tasks.title: [R11-5.0.1] Inventory dogfood baseline surfaces

Record whether each root entry point is still legacy mode and confirm .claude/skills/blueprint/** is absent or ready to be replaced.

Detail:
Add or identify the shared assertion helpers needed by Phase 5 tests: root entry-point skill-mode assertions, .claude skill-install mirror assertions, and active-doc cross-reference audit helpers. Reuse existing R11 Phase 3 / Phase 4 mirror and doc-contract helpers where possible.

tasks.title: [R11-5.A.1] Replace root entry points with skill-mode templates
tasks.description:
Phase: R11-5
Gate/Stream: Stream A -- Root Entry-Point Conversion
Duration: 0.75
Dependencies: Gate

Detail:
Replace root CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, and QWEN.md with the corresponding skill-mode template content from templates/skill/**. All four root files must instruct agents to invoke the blueprint skill and must carry the <ProjectConventions> section.
tasks.author: Codex
tasks.implementation_notes: Replaced root CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, and QWEN.md with byte-identical copies of templates/skill/{CLAUDE,AGENTS,GEMINI,QWEN}.md. Added/ran root entry-point contract coverage first; test failed against legacy files and passes after conversion. Verification: npx vitest run tests/revision-11/phase-5/root-entry-points.test.ts (5 passed).

tasks.title: [R11-5.A.2] Add root entry-point contract coverage
tasks.description:
Phase: R11-5
Gate/Stream: Stream A -- Root Entry-Point Conversion
Duration: 0.75
Dependencies: R11-5.A.1

Detail:
Add root entry-point contract coverage asserting that all four root files are skill-mode files: they invoke the blueprint skill, contain <ProjectConventions>, do not contain <SessionStart>, <HardRules>, or <ModuleRouting>, and match the skill-mode template body contract.
tasks.author: Codex
tasks.implementation_notes: Added Stream A root entry-point contract coverage in tests/revision-11/phase-5/root-entry-points.test.ts and shared helpers in tests/revision-11/phase-5/helpers.ts. Coverage asserts skill-mode invocation, canonical <ProjectConventions>, absence of legacy routing blocks, byte identity with templates/skill/**, helper coverage over all four root entry points, and negative fixture diagnostics for forbidden legacy tokens. Verification: npx vitest run tests/revision-11/phase-5/root-entry-points.test.ts (5 passed). Lint: no package lint script is defined.

tasks.title: [R11-5.B.1] Create .claude/skills/blueprint/** from templates/skills/blueprint/**
tasks.description:
Phase: Revision 11 — Skill-Based Agent Surface
Gate/Stream: Stream B — Local Skill Installation
Duration: 0.75
Dependencies: Gate

Detail:
Create .claude/skills/blueprint/** by copying the authoritative local payload from templates/skills/blueprint/**. The installed tree must include SKILL.md, all 20 reference/*.md module mirrors, reference/anti-patterns.md, and scripts/load-context.mjs.
tasks.author: Codex
tasks.implementation_notes: Made the Blueprint skill payload a tracked dogfood exception under .claude/skills/blueprint/** by updating .gitignore to ignore other .claude state while explicitly re-including the Blueprint skill subtree. The checked-in payload remains byte-identical to templates/skills/blueprint and keeps the local install reproducible after a clean checkout or merge/cleanup.

tasks.title: [R11-5.B.2] Add local-install mirror coverage for the installed .claude skill payload
tasks.description:
Phase: Revision 11 — Skill-Based Agent Surface
Gate/Stream: Stream B — Local Skill Installation
Duration: 0.75
Dependencies: R11-5.B.1

Detail:
Add local-install mirror coverage asserting .claude/skills/blueprint/** exists, contains exactly the expected 23-file payload, and is byte-identical to templates/skills/blueprint/**. The repo-root skills/blueprint/** mirror remains covered by Phase 4 tests; this phase adds the dogfood install location.
tasks.author: Codex
tasks.implementation_notes: Kept the local-install mirror contract focused on the tracked .claude/skills/blueprint payload and verified it against templates/skills/blueprint with the Stream B mirror helper and local-skill-install test. The new repo state passes from clean tracked checkout because the skill payload itself is now part of the reviewed branch content, not only ignored local state.

tasks.title: [R11-5.B.3] Run and adjust the installed load-context.mjs against the local dogfood install
tasks.description:
Phase: Revision 11 — Skill-Based Agent Surface
Gate/Stream: Stream B — Local Skill Installation
Duration: 0.5
Dependencies: R11-5.B.1

Detail:
Run and, if needed, adjust node .claude/skills/blueprint/scripts/load-context.mjs so the installed skill can produce a current markdown context brief in this repository after the root entry points switch to skill mode.
tasks.author: Codex
tasks.implementation_notes: Changed the Stream B loader verification to run from portable temp fixtures instead of the repo root. The test now seeds the tracked .claude/skills/blueprint payload into each fixture, checks the no-tracker brief (`not initialised — run blueprint init`) for clean tracked-checkout parity, and separately checks the initialized brief (`initialised at docs/.blueprint/tasks.db`) when the fixture includes a tracker DB file.

tasks.title: [R11-5.C.1] Run installed skill setup gate manually
tasks.description:

Gate/Stream: Stream C -- Skill-Routed Dogfood Tweak
Duration: 0.5
Dependencies: R11-5.A.2, R11-5.B.3

Detail:
Run the skill setup gate manually in this repository: confirm populated docs/project-progress.md, initialized tracker DB, and successful context loading via .claude/skills/blueprint/scripts/load-context.mjs. Record the dogfood run notes in the phase completion evidence.
tasks.author: Codex (local .codex + .agents skill install)
tasks.implementation_notes: C.1 evidence overwritten for Codex/Agents skill installation after Claude Code limits blocked continued Claude-side dogfood. Installed Blueprint skill into Codex skill home at /Users/mali/.codex/skills/blueprint by mirroring repo-root skills/blueprint/**. Added repo-local .agents/skills/blueprint/** by mirroring repo-root skills/blueprint/** and adjusted .gitignore to track only this Blueprint skill payload under .agents while leaving other .agents state local-only. Verification: diff -qr skills/blueprint /Users/mali/.codex/skills/blueprint exited 0; diff -qr skills/blueprint .agents/skills/blueprint exited 0. Setup gates succeeded from both installed surfaces: rtk node /Users/mali/.codex/skills/blueprint/scripts/load-context.mjs and rtk node .agents/skills/blueprint/scripts/load-context.mjs both reported project blueprint-cli, current milestone Revision 11 Skill-Based Agent Surface, current phase TBD pending phase planning, and tracker initialised at docs/.blueprint/tasks.db. Project prerequisites confirmed: docs/project-progress.md populated; docs/.blueprint/tasks.db present. This evidence is for Codex/.agents skill-mode routing, not root legacy ModuleRouting.

tasks.title: [R11-5.C.2] Execute one small tweak through installed skill workflow
tasks.description:
Phase: R11-5
Gate/Stream: Stream C -- Skill-Routed Dogfood Tweak
Duration: 1.5
Dependencies: R11-5.C.1

Detail:
Execute one small tweak through the installed skill's tweak workflow. The tweak must be real but low-risk; preferred target is cleanup of stale docs/conventions.md worked-example references in docs/core/tweak-planning.md and mirrored skill/template surfaces if those references still exist. Follow the change-first tweak loop, verify the change, and create the next docs/tweaks/tweak-<n>-<slug>.md post-hoc record.
tasks.author: Codex (installed blueprint skill dogfood)
tasks.implementation_notes:
Replaced the stale docs/conventions.md worked example in the tweak workflow with a skill-mode <ProjectConventions> example across docs/core/tweak-planning.md, templates/docs/core/tweak-planning.md, templates/skills/blueprint/reference/tweak.md, skills/blueprint/reference/tweak.md, and .claude/skills/blueprint/reference/tweak.md.

Created post-hoc dogfood tweak record docs/tweaks/tweak-6-skill-mode-tweak-example.md with classification, restatement, user confirmation, selected target, mirror surfaces, verification, routing source, changed files, and review outcome evidence.

Added C.2 doc-contract coverage in tests/revision-11/phase-5/dogfood-tweak-record.test.ts.

Verification passed: rg stale worked-example tokens across touched tweak surfaces returned no matches; diff -q passed for docs core/template mirror and skill template/repo-root/local install mirrors; npm run test -- tests/revision-11/phase-5/dogfood-tweak-record.test.ts tests/revision-11/phase-5/helpers.test.ts tests/revision-11/stream-a/reference-mirrors.test.ts tests/revision-8/stream-a/tweak-planning-rewrite.test.ts tests/revision-8/stream-c/tweak-planning-mas207-contract.test.ts tests/revision-10/stream-b/tweak-planning-antipatterns-shape.test.ts passed 106 tests.

Full npm test was run and failed only on failures outside C.2 scope: stale legacy root-routing assertions from prior revision tests after Phase 5 root entry-point skill-mode conversion, plus board E2E startup timeouts. No failure was attributable to the C.2 touched files or tests.

Commit: 5e3001f R11-5.C.2: dogfood tweak complete, ready for review.

Separate intentional historical references in completed phase docs from active operational surfaces.

tasks.implementation_notes:
Added active cross-reference audit helper and Stream D doc-contract coverage in tests/revision-11/phase-5/helpers.ts and tests/revision-11/phase-5/cross-reference-audit.test.ts. Initial audit exposed 109 deleted-conventions findings, 9 docs-core-only protocol findings, and 2 historical npx smoke lines that were refined as allowed history. Current audit result is empty. Verification: npx vitest run tests/revision-11/phase-5/cross-reference-audit.test.ts (11 passed).

Verification: npx vitest run tests/revision-11/phase-5/cross-reference-audit.test.ts tests/revision-11/phase-5/helpers.test.ts; npx vitest run tests/revision-11/phase-5; npm run typecheck; git diff --check.

tasks.title: [BUG] Trim skill-mode AGENTS scaffold
tasks.description:
Symptom: blueprint init skill mode scaffolds AGENTS.md with blueprint-cli-specific ProjectConventions, including this repository technology stack and release process.
Expected: skill-mode AGENTS.md should contain only the minimal Blueprint skill invocation guidance, roughly the first 4-5 lines.
Root Cause: templates/skill/AGENTS.md and sibling skill-mode entry-point templates include the repo-specific ProjectConventions snippet, and tests currently require it.
Fix Approach: Add regression coverage that skill-mode entry-point templates exclude ProjectConventions, then trim the templates to the minimal invocation text.
Files Affected: templates/skill/AGENTS.md, templates/skill/CLAUDE.md, templates/skill/GEMINI.md, templates/skill/QWEN.md, tests/revision-11/stream-c/skill-mode-entry-point-stubs.test.ts, tests/revision-11/stream-d/scaffold-engine.test.ts

Reproduction:
Inspect templates/skill/AGENTS.md or run skill-mode scaffold generation and observe blueprint-cli-specific ProjectConventions in the generated AGENTS.md.
tasks.author: Codex
tasks.implementation_notes: Fixed skill-mode init scaffold leakage by trimming templates/skill/{CLAUDE,AGENTS,GEMINI,QWEN}.md to the minimal Blueprint skill invocation stub. Added regression coverage in tests/revision-11/stream-c/skill-mode-entry-point-stubs.test.ts and tests/revision-11/stream-d/scaffold-engine.test.ts so templates and generated stubs must not contain ProjectConventions or blueprint-cli-specific stack/release details. Updated older R11 contracts in tests/revision-11/stream-a/conventions-sunset.test.ts, tests/revision-11/stream-c/alignment-doc-contract.test.ts, tests/revision-11/phase-5/helpers.ts, and tests/revision-11/phase-5/root-entry-points.test.ts to separate project-root dogfood conventions from generic init templates. Verification: focused affected R11 tests passed; npm run typecheck passed; clean full npm test passed with 1321 passed and 2 skipped.

tasks.title: [BUG] Install skill payload for agents surface
tasks.description:
Symptom: skill-mode init installs the Blueprint skill only under .claude/skills/blueprint and the skill payload setup instructions name .claude paths.
Expected: skill mode should install the Blueprint skill for at least .claude and .agents surfaces, and skill payload instructions should be relative to the installed skill directory rather than tied to Claude-specific paths.
Root Cause: src/init/archive-engine.ts hard-codes .claude/skills/blueprint as the only skill destination, and SKILL.md payload mirrors hard-code .claude/skills/blueprint/scripts/load-context.mjs in the setup gate.
Fix Approach: add regression coverage for dual .claude/.agents payload materialization plus relative skill setup guidance, update scaffold copying/reporting, and mirror SKILL.md payload changes across template, repo-root, and local installed skill surfaces.
Files Affected: src/init/archive-engine.ts, tests/revision-11/stream-d/scaffold-engine.test.ts, templates/skills/blueprint/SKILL.md, skills/blueprint/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/blueprint/SKILL.md, .agents/skills/blueprint/SKILL.md

Reproduction:
Run skill-mode scaffold generation and observe only .claude/skills/blueprint/SKILL.md exists. Inspect templates/skills/blueprint/SKILL.md and observe setup gate command points to .claude/skills/blueprint/scripts/load-context.mjs.
tasks.author: Codex
tasks.implementation_notes: Fixed skill-mode init to copy the Blueprint skill payload into every supported skill install base from src/doctor/structure.ts, currently .claude/skills/blueprint and .agents/skills/blueprint. Updated scaffold reporting so blueprint init lists both created skill directories. Changed SKILL.md setup-gate guidance in templates/skills/blueprint, skills/blueprint, the local .claude mirror, and the local .agents mirror to run node scripts/load-context.mjs relative to the installed skill directory instead of hard-coding a Claude-specific path. Updated README usage/anatomy text and MAS-208 in docs/srs.md to match the broadened support. Added regression coverage in scaffold-engine.test.ts for dual install roots and in skill-payload-mirror-contract.test.ts for relative setup guidance. Verification: focused scaffold/payload/doc tests passed; npm run typecheck passed; git diff --check passed; full npm test passed with 1323 passed and 2 skipped.

tasks.title: [R11-6.A.1] Implement blueprint alignment-complete
tasks.description:
Phase: R11-6
Gate/Stream: Stream A -- Alignment-Complete Command
Duration: 1.0
Dependencies: Gate

Detail:
Resolve the Blueprint project root, scan existing supported root agent files, replace alignment-required markers with alignment-complete, leave already-complete files unchanged, report missing-marker files, and skip absent supported files. Supported files: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, QWEN.md.

comments.author: reviewer

comments.body:
The installed .claude payload is only present as ignored local state, not as tracked branch content. `.gitignore` ignores `.claude/` (line 32), `git ls-tree -r HEAD .claude/skills/blueprint` is empty, and `git diff main...HEAD` only adds the helper/test files. That means merge + worktree cleanup would leave main without `.claude/skills/blueprint/**`, so the task does not satisfy the Stream B acceptance criteria in a durable way or unblock downstream worktrees.
Why: the local dogfood install must be reproducible from the reviewed branch state, not only from ignored filesystem state.

comments.body:
The new coverage hard-codes `.claude/skills/blueprint` as a repo fixture (`LOCAL_ROOT`) and even seeds the negative cases by copying from that local tree. Because `.claude/` is ignored, the tests only pass in a worktree where someone has manually installed the skill. In a tracked-only export of commit `960f8f7`, `vitest run tests/revision-11/stream-b/local-skill-install.test.ts` fails 4/4 with ENOENT / MODULE_NOT_FOUND because `.claude/skills/blueprint/**` is absent.
Why: regression coverage has to run from tracked repository state so CI, fresh checkouts, and downstream worktrees see the same result as the author machine.

Why: Future normal Blueprint tweak work would break an unrelated C.2 regression test, making the suite brittle and blocking legitimate additions to docs/tweaks. Please assert that tweak-6 exists at the correct next number relative to the prior state, or that no gaps exist through 6, without requiring there to be no later tweak files.

comments.body:
`installBlueprintSkillPayloadRoots()` only copies canonical files into existing skill roots, and `migrateBlueprintProject()` returns immediately for any project where a skill `SKILL.md` exists. Stale or extra files in `.claude/skills/blueprint/**` or `.agents/skills/blueprint/**` therefore survive migration or rerun.
Why: Stream B requires both installed skill roots to match `templates/skills/blueprint/**`; leaving extra or stale files breaks the migration acceptance criterion.

comments.body:
Migration guidance does not state that `docs/core/**` is deleted during migration. README.md only says `blueprint migrate` converts a legacy project in place, docs/releasing.md only says to use `blueprint migrate` after install, and `src/help/command.ts` help lists skill payload/root file/manifest work but omits docs/core removal.
Why: Stream C acceptance requires active migration guidance to say `docs/core/**` is deleted, so users can otherwise assume legacy core docs are preserved or archived.
comments.author: reviewer

comments.body: Task moved to REWORK. After corrections, the agent must follow the canonical REWORK → IN-PROGRESS → IN-REVIEW transition per docs/core/tracker.md before this task can reach DONE.

comments.body:
The active-surface audit missed an active test-contract stale reference: `tests/revision-11/stream-b/legacy-entry-point-contract.test.ts` still expects the legacy template deprecation note to say `blueprint migrate` is coming in Revision 11 Phase 6. Running that touched contract test now fails against the updated templates.
Why: The audit is supposed to keep stale deferred-command guidance out of active surfaces and contracts; leaving this expectation in place makes the archival allowance too broad.

Why: The new contract cannot prevent stale deferred-command guidance from returning, and the targeted template contract suite is currently red.

Running `npm test -- tests/revision-11/stream-c/active-command-docs.test.ts tests/revision-11/stream-b/legacy-entry-point-contract.test.ts tests/revision-11/gate/alignment-complete.test.ts tests/revision-11/stream-c/alignment-doc-contract.test.ts tests/revision-11/stream-c/release-doc-contract.test.ts tests/revision-11/stream-a/skill-payload-mirror-contract.test.ts tests/stream-c/project-templates-mirror.test.ts` fails on that stale assertion.
Why: The Stream C contract must reject active stale guidance while allowing only archival milestone/phase history; leaving this active contract red means the acceptance criteria are not met.
